sources.latex_brief_root: /Users/crpage/repos/mcp-geo/research/From Apps to Answers - Connecting Public Sector Data to AI with MCP

standards.title: Guidelines for Secure AI System Development
Guidance on AI and data protection
About the Secure Research Service (Five Safes Framework)

compliance_checklists.statement: Every user-facing answer path includes provenance metadata (source, transform, limits).
compliance_checklists.verification: Inspect response payload constructors and report generators; assert provenance block presence in tests.

compliance_checklists.statement: Every high-impact workflow has explicit deny-safe defaults and fallback labeling.
compliance_checklists.verification: Inspect routing and guardrail code paths; verify explicit default behavior and warning fields.

compliance_checklists.statement: Tool routes enforce intent-to-workflow mapping before heavy data calls.
compliance_checklists.verification: Review route handlers for intent classification and sequencing constraints.

compliance_checklists.statement: Payload budgets and progressive disclosure are enforced in code, not only in docs.
compliance_checklists.verification: Check limits, chunking, resource handoff logic, and tests for large responses.

compliance_checklists.statement: Sensitive datasets require explicit authorization checks before retrieval.
compliance_checklists.verification: Inspect endpoint/tool handlers for authz gates and policy checks.

compliance_checklists.statement: Sensitive responses default to aggregation/redaction unless explicitly authorized.
compliance_checklists.verification: Inspect response shaping and policy hooks; run tests over restricted datasets.

compliance_checklists.statement: All secret-bearing config fields and token-like keys are redacted from logs/errors.
compliance_checklists.verification: Inject secret fixtures and assert they never appear in log/output snapshots.

compliance_checklists.statement: Audit logs are structured and replayable into deterministic timelines.
compliance_checklists.verification: Replay trace-to-report pipeline and compare with runtime events.

compliance_checklists.statement: Correlation IDs are propagated across request, tool calls, and responses.
compliance_checklists.verification: Trace correlation IDs through middleware, transports, and tool-call records.

compliance_checklists.statement: Provenance structure maps to PROV model concepts (entity/activity/agent).
compliance_checklists.verification: Inspect provenance payload schema and mapping docs.

compliance_checklists.statement: Automation decisions can be documented and reviewed against ATRS expectations.
compliance_checklists.verification: Check fields needed for ATRS records and assurance reporting.

compliance_checklists.statement: File/path resource access is traversal-safe with canonical containment checks.
compliance_checklists.verification: Run traversal tests over all file-backed resource branches.

compliance_checklists.statement: Client error payloads do not expose raw internal exception details.
compliance_checklists.verification: Trigger exceptions and inspect client-visible error envelopes.

compliance_checklists.statement: Rate limits, retries, and degraded-mode fallbacks are secure-by-default.
compliance_checklists.verification: Validate config defaults and runtime behavior under pressure tests.

compliance_checklists.statement: Resource metadata is catalog-compatible with DCAT v3 semantics.
compliance_checklists.verification: Check catalog fields and mapping in resource descriptors.

compliance_checklists.statement: MCP transport behavior aligns with dated spec revision 2025-11-25.
compliance_checklists.verification: Compare protocol handlers with spec method/shape requirements.

compliance_checklists.statement: Governance claims in research outputs are cited to UK/standards authorities.
compliance_checklists.verification: Audit claim-to-citation mapping and bibliography metadata integrity.

revisions.title: Harden file resource path containment
revisions.todo: #TODO# Completed 2026-02-21: Replaced string-prefix checks with canonical scoped resolution/containment checks and disallowed encoded/mixed separator traversal tokens.
details: Fix branches: ons-cache, ons-exports, os-cache, os-exports, exports.
Normalize before existence checks.
Reject traversal and sibling-prefix escapes with INVALID_INPUT envelope.
acceptance_criteria: Traversal payloads (../, encoded separators, sibling-prefix) are rejected for every file-backed resource URI.
Existing valid resource reads remain functional.
No branch uses string prefix containment checks.

revisions.title: Add traversal regression tests for resource handlers
revisions.todo: #TODO# Completed 2026-02-21: Added traversal regression coverage for ons-cache/ons-exports/os-cache/os-exports/exports plus sibling-prefix and encoded/mixed separator cases.
details: Cover on-disk branches: ons-cache, ons-exports, os-cache, os-exports, exports.
Include sibling-prefix and mixed path separator cases.
acceptance_criteria: All malicious path test cases return INVALID_INPUT or NOT_FOUND without out-of-root file reads.
CI includes these tests in default pytest suite.

revisions.title: Expand secrets redaction scope
revisions.todo: #TODO# Completed 2026-02-21: Added configured secret collection (OS + NOMIS) and key-name-based masking for signature/token/api_key/authorization-style fields.
details: Include NOMIS_UID and NOMIS_SIGNATURE in redaction seed.
Apply key-name-based masking for signature/token/api_key/authorization keys.
Ensure exception handler masking uses complete secret set.
acceptance_criteria: No known credential appears in logs or API error payloads.
Redaction behavior is consistent for nested dict/list values.

revisions.title: Add log redaction verification tests
revisions.todo: #TODO# Completed 2026-02-21: Added regression tests for upstream-log masking, nested token-like key masking, and generic exception redaction output.
details: Cover upstream error logging with params payloads.
Cover generic exception handler output.
Cover nested structures.
acceptance_criteria: Test fixtures with secret values never appear in captured log output.
Mask token is consistently applied.

revisions.title: Sanitize internal exception responses in transports
revisions.todo: #TODO# Completed 2026-02-21: Replaced raw exception leakage with generic `Internal error` responses and per-error correlation IDs in STDIO and HTTP transports.
details: Keep full exception details only in server logs.
Use consistent JSON-RPC code mapping for internal errors.
acceptance_criteria: Client responses never include raw exception text.
Operational logs still allow diagnosis via correlation ID.

revisions.title: Normalize malformed JSON handling to 400 envelopes
revisions.todo: #TODO# Completed 2026-02-21: Added local malformed JSON guards for tools/playground endpoints with deterministic HTTP 400 INVALID_INPUT envelopes.
details: Guard `await request.json()` with parse exception handling.
Ensure response shape matches repository error model.
acceptance_criteria: Malformed JSON requests return HTTP 400 with deterministic error envelope.
No malformed JSON case reaches generic 500 handler.

revisions.title: Use secure rate-limit defaults
revisions.todo: #TODO# Completed 2026-02-21: Set secure RATE_LIMIT_BYPASS default to false, added explicit test/dev overrides, and updated environment/docs guidance.
details: Align defaults with secure production posture.
Keep test fixtures toggling bypass explicitly where required.
acceptance_criteria: Default runtime enforces limiter without extra configuration.
Tests/dev docs remain reproducible with explicit bypass config.

revisions.title: Fix logging format consistency and debug print
revisions.todo: #TODO# Completed 2026-02-21: Removed raw startup print and migrated main request/error logging to loguru-compatible `{}` formatting.
details: Ensure all audit log lines render structured fields consistently.
Route startup diagnostics through logger only.
acceptance_criteria: No `%s` placeholder logging remains in main request/error paths.
Startup message appears through configured logger with redaction patcher.

revisions.title: Resolve residual uncited governance statements in brief
files: research/From Apps to Answers - Connecting Public Sector Data to AI with MCP/sections/01-executive-summary.tex
research/From Apps to Answers - Connecting Public Sector Data to AI with MCP/sections/02-problem-and-thesis.tex
revisions.todo: #TODO# Completed 2026-02-21: Added standards citations to executive summary assurance sentence and governed middle-layer thesis sentence.
details: Prefer PROV/ATRS for auditability claims.
Prefer NCSC/Data and AI Ethics Framework for governance assertions.
acceptance_criteria: All core governance/safety claims in summary/thesis sections have explicit standards citations.
No document-structure rewrite required.

revisions.title: Verify internal artefact URL for codex trace citation
files: research/From Apps to Answers - Connecting Public Sector Data to AI with MCP/references.bib
revisions.todo: #TODO# Completed 2026-02-21: Replaced local `file://` codex trace citation URL with stable repository evidence reference.
details: Current key: artefact_codex_trace_jsonl_2026_02_18.
Use canonical repository location or signed archival reference.
acceptance_criteria: Citation resolves from external review context.
Metadata note accurately reflects access constraints if private.

transition_plan.objective: Sustain fully_met by keeping blocking controls regression-tested and monitoring low-priority evidence hygiene.
fully_met_criteria: All blocking high/medium revisions are complete and test-covered.
No known traversal path remains in resource handlers.
No secret leaks in logs/errors under credential-bearing test fixtures.
No transport/client response includes raw internal exception text.
Governance claim-to-citation mapping has no unresolved needs-improvement items.
